title: Platform Architecture — UPM Agentic SDLC Factory
subtitle: Hybrid A+C approach · Microsoft-native SaaS for quick wins + Custom Azure AI Foundry agents for complex capabilities
strategyNote: Phase 1 (H2 2026): Deploy 6 capabilities via SaaS + custom agents. Phase 2 (H1 2027): Consolidate to unified platform, add capabilities 7–11.

layers.name: UPM AI CoE Control Plane
layers.description: Centralized governance, routing, and policy enforcement

components.name: AI Gateway
components.detail: Azure API Management — centralized LLM routing, rate limiting, cost attribution

components.name: Responsible AI Policies
components.detail: Content safety filters, PII detection, output audit logging

components.name: Agent Registry
components.detail: A2A agent cards — discovery, versioning, capability metadata

components.name: Gate Approvals (G1–G4)
components.detail: Human-in-the-loop sign-off gates for sensitive actions

layers.name: Orchestration Layer
layers.description: MS Agent Framework coordinates agent workflows and long-running tasks

components.name: Coordinator Agent
components.detail: Routes requests to capability agents, manages multi-step workflows

components.name: MS Agent Framework
components.detail: Agent lifecycle, communication protocol (A2A), state management

components.name: Azure Service Bus
components.detail: Async event routing between agents, dead-letter handling

components.name: Durable Functions
components.detail: Long-wait orchestrations (HITL gates, batch processing)

groups.label: SaaS-Accelerated (Phase 1 — Sprints 1–2)
groups.sublabel: Pre-built tools, fast deployment, familiar UX

groups.label: Custom-Built (Phase 1 — Sprints 3–7)
groups.sublabel: No SaaS fit — Azure AI Foundry + Semantic Kernel

layers.name: Knowledge Service
layers.description: Shared RAG backbone — write once, read by all agents

components.name: Azure AI Search
components.detail: Vector store — Confluence, SharePoint, architecture catalog, project history

components.name: Redis Cache
components.detail: Hot-path caching — 30–50% hit rate for repeated queries

components.name: Blob Storage
components.detail: Source documents, embeddings, versioned snapshots

components.name: Knowledge Curator
components.detail: Freshness scoring, gap detection, update recommendations

layers.dataFlow: Agents READ → Cache → miss → AI Search → Agents WRITE → Service Bus → Ingest

layers.name: Integration Layer
layers.description: Shared MCP connector library (built once, reused across all capabilities) — MCP-first, REST fallback, A2A bridges

layers.name: Observability & Audit
layers.description: Full OpenTelemetry trace pipeline — cost attribution, compliance audit

components.name: OpenTelemetry SDK
components.detail: Structured traces from every agent invocation

components.name: Azure Monitor
components.detail: Centralized log aggregation, alerting, dashboards

components.name: Application Insights
components.detail: Performance tracking, dependency mapping, anomaly detection

components.name: AI CoE Dashboards
components.detail: Cost per agent, token usage, quality scores, SLA compliance

layers.name: Infrastructure (Azure)
layers.description: Terraform IaC — repeatable, auditable, Azure DevOps CI/CD

components.name: Terraform IaC
components.detail: All infra as code — Container Apps, AI Search, Functions, Service Bus

components.name: Key Vault
components.detail: Secrets, certificates, API keys — Managed Identity access only

components.name: Entra ID
components.detail: Role-based access, Managed Identity for service-to-service auth

components.name: VNet + Private Endpoints
components.detail: Network isolation, no public endpoints for backend services

guardrails: No human sign-off = no publish / merge / deploy
All AI output logged and auditable via OpenTelemetry
Role-based data access via Entra ID
LLM access only through AI Gateway (no direct calls)
Python + Terraform for all custom code
New agents require AI CoE Architecture Review before production
Semgrep OSS licence scanning in all CI/CD pipelines (Black Duck TBD Q4 2026)
Responsible AI review (bias, fairness, privacy, explainability) per agent at Architecture Review
